explanation: Bypasses by renaming chisel.exe to update.exe (avoiding selection_img) and using '-p' flag instead of the monitored param2 patterns like '-socks5', '-reverse', etc.
Uses only param1 pattern ('exe client') without any param2 patterns (no -socks5, -reverse, etc.), so the 'all of selection_param*' condition fails.
Uses PowerShell encoded command to hide the chisel execution parameters from direct command line monitoring.
Uses DLL version of chisel instead of executable, and uses different syntax that doesn't match the detection patterns.
